generatedSentence: At daybreak, the nonconformist astronomer, deep in melancholy solitude, often observed the outer space majesty through his telescope, finding newfound romantic moments among the stars, a dorky outcast among the rejects and nobodies, often consuming a grotesque oatmeal from a fluffed napkin, his hoarse voice reciting a magical parable that seemed to injunct the very kilojoule of the universe, hiding within the city's artistic undergrounds. The infamous warlord, known for his blasphemy and undependable nature, often used tourism as a front for his helicopter operations, relying on jokers and tipoffs to carjack targets for an elusive formula, while his liaison covertly joined the viceroy's ranks to probe and uncover a vast jackpot, ready to betray anyone who wouldn't obey, even if it meant perjury and a massive gaffe, his voice growing louder as the iconic goujons of power began to oxidize under his grip, threatening to kneecap his ambition. He jumped from the van's landing ramp, an unexpected flashback of princesses and a jigsaw puzzle atlas filling his mind, preparing for the upcoming confrontation where an official, now eloquent with a layer of wealth, had set a trap – a high-stakes game of blackjack that sickened him, but he anyways punched the keypad, breaking through the drag, forcing a lockjaw on the guards, yelling 'Hooray!' aloud as the flame of revolution counters their plans, smashing ceramics and coconuts in his path. Through endless nightmares and memories, he learned to persevere, wisely adopting a nonchalant demeanor as the outflow of power shifted, allowing him to occupy a new role; a neophyte with rubber gauntlets, strumming a ukulele by a thatched hut, a seagull his only companion, believing the magical djembes and junkets of wealth would now fund a lifeboat for nobodies, not just the privileged, finally at peace, despite the occasional grotesque thought of oatmeal and kilojoules.
panelSentences: At daybreak, the nonconformist astronomer, deep in melancholy solitude, often observed the outer space majesty through his telescope, finding newfound romantic moments among the stars, a dorky outcast among the rejects and nobodies, often consuming a grotesque oatmeal from a fluffed napkin, his hoarse voice reciting a magical parable that seemed to injunct the very kilojoule of the universe, hiding within the city's artistic undergrounds.

The infamous warlord, known for his blasphemy and undependable nature, often used tourism as a front for his helicopter operations, relying on jokers and tipoffs to carjack targets for an elusive formula, while his liaison covertly joined the viceroy's ranks to probe and uncover a vast jackpot, ready to betray anyone who wouldn't obey, even if it meant perjury and a massive gaffe, his voice growing louder as the iconic goujons of power began to oxidize under his grip, threatening to kneecap his ambition.

He jumped from the van's landing ramp, an unexpected flashback of princesses and a jigsaw puzzle atlas filling his mind, preparing for the upcoming confrontation where an official, now eloquent with a layer of wealth, had set a trap – a high-stakes game of blackjack that sickened him, but he anyways punched the keypad, breaking through the drag, forcing a lockjaw on the guards, yelling 'Hooray!' aloud as the flame of revolution counters their plans, smashing ceramics and coconuts in his path.

The infamous warlord, known for his blasphemy and undependable nature, often used tourism as a front for his helicopter operations, relying on jokers and tipoffs to carjack targets for an elusive formula, while his liaison covertly joined the viceroy's ranks to probe and uncover a vast jackpot, ready to betray anyone who wouldn't obey, even if it meant perjury and a massive gaffe, his voice growing louder as the iconic goujons of power began to oxidize under his grip, threatening to kneecap his ambition.

He jumped from the van's landing ramp, an unexpected flashback of princesses and a jigsaw puzzle atlas filling his mind, preparing for the upcoming confrontation where an official, now eloquent with a layer of wealth, had set a trap – a high-stakes game of blackjack that sickened him, but he anyways punched the keypad, breaking through the drag, forcing a lockjaw on the guards, yelling 'Hooray!' aloud as the flame of revolution counters their plans, smashing ceramics and coconuts in his path.

Through endless nightmares and memories, he learned to persevere, wisely adopting a nonchalant demeanor as the outflow of power shifted, allowing him to occupy a new role; a neophyte with rubber gauntlets, strumming a ukulele by a thatched hut, a seagull his only companion, believing the magical djembes and junkets of wealth would now fund a lifeboat for nobodies, not just the privileged, finally at peace, despite the occasional grotesque thought of oatmeal and kilojoules.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lone figure wearing a high-tech, glowing trench coat and a visor helmet with a single emerald eye. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Lone protagonist observes a distant nebula through a large, ornate telescope, silhouetted against a futuristic cityscape at daybreak, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An armored helicopter descends onto a rooftop landing pad, a menacing warlord gesturing aggressively from the cockpit, while a shadowy figure hands a data chip to a viceroy, wide shot from below. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist leaps from a speeding, armored van, smashing a glowing keypad on a reinforced door, surrounded by sparks and shattered ceramic shards, dynamic low-angle action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, now relaxed, plays a glowing ukulele on a futuristic thatched beach hut, a robotic seagull perched nearby, gazing at a serene, alien ocean, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A style of ornamentation characterized by fanciful combinations of intertwined forms.
Anything grotesque.
A sans serif typeface.
Distorted and unnatural in shape or size; abnormal and hideous.
Disgusting or otherwise viscerally revolting.
Sans serif.
A member of a church separated from the Church of England; a Protestant dissenter.
Loosely, a Christian who does not conform to the doctrines of an established church.
Someone who does not conform to accepted beliefs, customs or practices.
Not conforming to established customs etc.
Film noir.
A production in the style of film noir.
Of or pertaining to film noir, or the atmosphere associated with that genre
Casually calm and relaxed.
definitions.example: We handled the whole frenetic situation with a nonchalant attitude.

definitions.definition: Indifferent; unconcerned; behaving as if detached.
definitions.example: He is far too nonchalant about such a serious matter.

definitions.definition: The quality of being impressive and great.
definitions.example: the majesty of the Great Pyramids

definitions.definition: Of or relating to magic.
Enchanting.
definitions.example: The fireworks created a magical atmosphere in that beautiful summer night.

definitions.definition: At depth, in a deep way.
To a deep extent.
Profoundly.
An intersection of piping or wiring; an interconnect.
An intersection of data in two or more database tables.
The act of joining something, such as a network.
The lowest upper bound, an operation between pairs of elements in a lattice, denoted by the symbol ∨.
To connect or combine into one; to put together.
definitions.example: The plumber joined the two ends of the broken pipe.

definitions.definition: To come together; to meet.
definitions.example: Parallel lines never join.

definitions.definition: To come into the company of.
definitions.example: I will join you watching the football game as soon as I have finished my work.

definitions.definition: To become a member of.
definitions.example: Many children join a sports club.

definitions.definition: To produce an intersection of data in two or more database tables.
definitions.example: By joining the Customer table on the Product table, we can show each customer's name alongside the products they have ordered.

definitions.definition: To unite in marriage.
To enjoin upon; to command.
To accept, or engage in, as a contest.
definitions.example: to join encounter, battle, or issue

definitions.definition: An aircraft that is borne along by one or more sets of long rotating blades which allow it to hover, move in any direction including reverse, or land; and typically having a smaller set of blades on its tail that stabilize the aircraft.
definitions.example: We flew over the city in a helicopter.

definitions.definition: A powered troweling machine with spinning blades used to spread concrete.
The winged fruit of certain trees, such as ash, elm, and maple.
A dragonfly.
A whirling trick performed with devil sticks.
To transport by helicopter.
To travel by helicopter.
To rotate like a helicopter blade.
definitions.example: helicoptering his jacket, helicoptering his arms

definitions.definition: To overprotect one's children.
Relating to, or having the characteristics of, an icon.
Famously and distinctively representative of its type.
Representing something; symbolic.
definitions.example: an iconic gesture in sign language

definitions.definition: To deliver into the hands of an enemy by treachery or fraud, in violation of trust; to give up treacherously or faithlessly.
definitions.example: an officer betrayed the city

definitions.definition: To prove faithless or treacherous to, as to a trust or one who trusts; to be false to; to deceive.
definitions.example: My eyes have been betraying me since I turned sixty.

definitions.definition: To violate the confidence of, by disclosing a secret, or that which one is bound in honor not to make known.
To disclose or indicate, for example something which prudence would conceal; to reveal unintentionally.
definitions.example: Though he had lived in England for many years, a faint accent betrayed his Swedish origin.

definitions.definition: To mislead; to expose to inconvenience not foreseen; to lead into error or sin.
To lead astray; to seduce (as under promise of marriage) and then abandon.
The act of travelling or sightseeing, particularly away from one's home.
The industry in which such travels and sightseeing are organized.
Collectively, the tourists visiting a place or landmark.
The act of visiting another region or jurisdiction for a particular purpose.
definitions.example: libel tourism; suicide tourism; sex tourism

definitions.definition: An obvious clue or indication.
definitions.example: The broken window and overturned plant pots were a tip-off that something was wrong.

definitions.definition: A report of suspicious behaviour, especially to an authority.
definitions.example: The police received a tip-off about a recent bank robbery.

definitions.definition: Not expected, anticipated or foreseen.
To remove a cover from.
definitions.example: The model railway was uncovered.

definitions.definition: To reveal the identity of.
definitions.example: The murderer has finally been uncovered.

definitions.definition: To show openly; to disclose; to reveal.
To remove one's hat or cap as a mark of respect.
To expose the genitalia.
To expose (lines of formation of troops) successively by the wheeling to right or left of the lines in front.
Any mathematical rule expressed symbolically.
A symbolic expression of the structure of a compound.
definitions.example: H2O is the formula for water.

definitions.definition: A plan or method for dealing with a problem or for achieving a result.
definitions.example: The company's winning formula includes excellent service and quality products.

definitions.definition: A formulation; a prescription; a mixture or solution made in a prescribed manner; the identity and quantities of ingredients of such a mixture.
definitions.example: The formula of the rocket fuel has not been revealed.

definitions.definition: A formal statement of doctrine, as in religion.

A syntactic expression of a proposition, built up from quantifiers, logical connectives, variables, relation and operation symbols, and, depending on the type of logic, possibly other operators such as modal, temporal, deontic or epistemic ones.
A manufactured food simulating human breast milk, designed to be fed to babies and infants under 12 months of age.
The flat, roundish bone in the knee.
(roofing) A metal cover trim that fits over a panel rib after it has been cut and bent.
A cap or strong covering for the knees, used chiefly for horses, to protect their knees in case of a fall.
To destroy the knees of (a person), usually by shooting at the knees, as a punishment carried out by criminals or terrorists.
A short narrative illustrating a lesson (usually religious/moral) by comparison or analogy.
definitions.example: Catholic sermons normally draw on at least one Biblical lecture, often parables.

definitions.definition: To represent by parable.
Any of various medical instruments used to explore wounds, organs, etc.
Something which penetrates something else, as though to explore; something which obtains information.
An act of probing; a prod, a poke.
An investigation or inquiry.

definitions.definition: A tube attached to an aircraft which can be fitted into the drogue from a tanker aircraft to allow for aerial refuelling.
A small device, especially an electrode, used to explore, investigate or measure something by penetrating or being placed in it.
definitions.example: Insert the probe into the soil and read the temperature.

definitions.definition: A small, usually unmanned, spacecraft used to acquire information or measurements about its surroundings.
(game of go) a move with multiple answers seeking to make the opponent choose and commit to a strategy
Any group of atoms or molecules radioactively labeled in order to study a given molecule or other structure

definitions.definition: To insert a probe into.
Recently found; newly discovered.
Someone who is not important or well-known.
definitions.example: Whether you're a nobody or a player, everyone in Tinseltown wants to be King of Hollywood! - Conquering Hollywood

definitions.definition: To descend to a surface, especially from the air.

definitions.definition: To alight, to descend from a vehicle.
To come into rest.
To arrive at land, especially a shore, or a dock, from a body of water.
To bring to land.

definitions.definition: To acquire; to secure.
To deliver.
Corridor.
Coming to a surface, as of an airplane or any descending object.
A place on a shoreline where a boat lands.
A level area at the top of a flight of stairs, or connecting one flight with another.
Resistance of the air (or some other fluid) to something moving through it.
definitions.example: When designing cars, manufacturers have to take drag into consideration.

definitions.definition: (foundry) The bottom part of a sand casting mold.
A device dragged along the bottom of a body of water in search of something, e.g. a dead body, or in fishing.
A puff on a cigarette or joint.
Someone or something that is annoying or frustrating, or disappointing; an obstacle to progress or enjoyment.

definitions.definition: A long open horse-drawn carriage with transverse or side seats.
Street, as in 'main drag'.
The scent-path left by dragging a fox, or some other substance such as aniseed, for training hounds to follow scents.

definitions.definition: A large amount of backspin on the cue ball, causing the cue ball to slow down.
A heavy harrow for breaking up ground.
A kind of sledge for conveying heavy objects; also, a kind of low car or handcart.
definitions.example: a stone drag

definitions.definition: The bottom part of a flask or mould, the upper part being the cope.
A steel instrument for completing the dressing of soft stone.
The difference between the speed of a screw steamer under sail and that of the screw when the ship outruns the screw; or between the propulsive effects of the different floats of a paddle wheel.
Anything towed in the water to retard a ship's progress, or to keep her head up to the wind; especially, a canvas bag with a hooped mouth (drag sail), so used.
A skid or shoe for retarding the motion of a carriage wheel.
Motion affected with slowness and difficulty, as if clogged.
Witch house music.
The last position in a line of hikers.
(aerodynamics) The act of suppressing wind flow to slow an aircraft in flight, as by use of flaps when landing.
A push somewhat under the centre of the cue ball, causing it to follow the object ball a short way.
A device for guiding wood to the saw.
A mailcoach.
To pull along a surface or through a medium, sometimes with difficulty.
definitions.example: Let's drag this load of wood over to the shed.

definitions.definition: To move onward heavily, laboriously, or slowly; to advance with weary effort; to go on lingeringly.
definitions.example: Time seems to drag when you’re waiting for a bus.

definitions.definition: To act or proceed slowly or without enthusiasm; to be reluctant.
To draw along (something burdensome); hence, to pass in pain or with difficulty.
To serve as a clog or hindrance; to hold back.
To move (an item) on the computer display by means of a mouse or other input device.
definitions.example: Drag the file into the window to open it.

definitions.definition: (chiefly of a vehicle) To unintentionally rub or scrape on a surface.

definitions.definition: To hit or kick off target.
To fish with a dragnet.
To search for something, as a lost object or body, by dragging something along the bottom of a body of water.
To break (land) by drawing a drag or harrow over it; to harrow.
To search exhaustively, as if with a dragnet.
To roast, say negative things about, or call attention to the flaws of (someone).

To cut something using a jigsaw.
Having a dry, harsh tone to the voice, as a result of a sore throat, age, emotion, etc.
A shout to signify victory.

Used to express approval, joy or victory.

Having a bad reputation, disreputable; notoriously bad, unpleasant or evil; widely known, especially for something bad.

An act of irreverence or contempt toward a god or toward something considered sacred; an impious act, utterance, view, etc.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An act of irreverence towards anything considered inviolable; the act of disregarding a convention.
An underground railway, especially for mass transit of people in urban areas.

A rapid transit system, regardless of the elevation of its right of way.
An underground walkway, tunnel for pedestrians (called pedestrian underpass in US).
An underground route for pipes, sewers, etc.
Regions beneath the surface of the earth, both natural (eg. caves) and man-made (eg. mines).
(with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist political convention.

definitions.definition: (with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist artistic convention.
To route electricity distribution cables underground
To do as ordered by (a person, institution etc), to act according to the bidding of.
To do as one is told.
To be obedient, compliant (to a given law, restriction etc.).
Region outside explored space.
Any region of space beyond limits determined with reference to boundaries of a celestial system or body, especially the region of space immediately beyond Earth's atmosphere.
A bluish shade of black.
Meal made from rolled or round oats.
A breakfast cereal made from rolled oats, cooked in milk and/or water.
A light greyish brown colour, like that of oatmeal.
Of a light greyish brown colour, like that of oatmeal
Spoken out loud.
With a loud voice, or great noise; loudly; audibly.

definitions.definition: Audibly, as opposed to silently/quietly.
definitions.example: speaking aloud rather than thinking thoughts privately

definitions.definition: (authorship) A dramatic device in which an earlier event is inserted into the normal chronological flow of a narrative.
A vivid mental image of a past trauma, especially one that recurs.
A similar recurrence of the effects of a hallucinogenic drug.
The condition of the flame propagating down the hose of an oxy-fuel welding system.
A query that operates against data from an earlier time, before it was changed.
To undergo a flashback; to experience a vivid mental image from the past.
A female member of a royal family other than a queen, especially a daughter or granddaughter.
A woman or girl who excels in a given field or class.
A female ruler or monarch; a queen.
The wife of a prince; the female ruler of a principality.

definitions.definition: A young girl; used as a term of endearment.
A young girl or woman (or less commonly a man) who is vain, spoiled or selfish; a prima donna.
A tinted crystal marble used in children's games.
A type of court card in the Tarot pack, coming between the 10 and the prince (Jack).
A female lemur.
One who governs a country, province, or colony as the representative of a monarch.
definitions.example: Ireland was governed by a Viceroy representing the English King/Queen when it was part of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland.

definitions.definition: A zongdu.
An orange and black North American butterfly (Limenitis archippus), so named because it is similar to, but smaller than, the monarch butterfly.
A mudcat (fish).
A thin strip of food, usually fish or chicken.
A serviette; a (usually rectangular) piece of cloth or paper used at the table for wiping the mouth and hands for cleanliness while eating.
A nappy (UK), a diaper (American).
A small scarf worn on the head by Christian women (chiefly Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox) when entering a church, as a token of modesty.
A sanitary napkin.
A force compounded by means of a lever rotating around a pivot; see torque.
definitions.example: A crowbar uses leverage to pry nails out of wood.

definitions.definition: By extension, any influence which is compounded or used to gain an advantage.
definitions.example: Try using competitors’ prices for leverage in the negotiation.

definitions.definition: The use of borrowed funds with a contractually determined return to increase the ability of a business to invest and earn an expected higher return, but usually at high risk.
definitions.example: Leverage is great until something goes wrong with your investments and you still have to pay your debts.

definitions.definition: The ability to earn very high returns when operating at high capacity utilization of a facility.
definitions.example: Their variable-cost-reducing investments have dramatically increased their leverage.

definitions.definition: To use; to exploit; to manipulate in order to take full advantage (of something).

definitions.definition: Communication between two parties or groups.
Co-operation, working together.
A relayer of information between two forces in an army or during war.
A tryst, romantic meeting.
An illicit sexual relationship or affair.

The phonological fusion of two consecutive words and the manner in which this occurs, for example intrusion, consonant-vowel linking, etc. In the context of some languages, such as French, liaison can refer specifically to a normally silent final consonant, being pronounced when the next word begins with a vowel, and can often also include the intrusion of a "t" in certain fixed chunks of language such as the question form "pense-t-il".
To liaise.
In a wise manner; using good judgement.
definitions.example: She wisely decided to re-check her homework before submitting it.

definitions.definition: Dawn.
A person who makes jokes.
A funny person.
A jester.
A playing card that features a picture of a joker (that is, a jester) and that may be used as a wild card in some card games.
An unspecified, vaguely disreputable person.
definitions.example: Some joker keeps throwing eggs at my windows.

definitions.definition: A man.
A clause in a contract that undermines its apparent provisions.
Having no end.

definitions.definition: Extending indefinitely.

definitions.definition: Without profitable end; fruitless; unsatisfying.
Aloneness; state of being alone or solitary, by oneself.
A lonely or deserted place.
Of or dealing with languages or cultures derived from Roman influence and Latin: Italian, French, Portuguese, Spanish, Romanian, Catalan, Occitan, Corsican, etc.
Of or pertaining to Romance.
Of or pertaining to Romanticism.
A person with romantic character (a character like those of the knights in a mythic romance).
A person who is behaving romantically (in a manner befitting someone who feels an idealized form of love).

definitions.definition: Of a work of literature, a writer etc.: being like or having the characteristics of a romance, or poetic tale of a mythic or quasi-historical time; fantastic.
Fictitious, imaginary.
Fantastic, unrealistic (of an idea etc.); fanciful, sentimental, impractical (of a person).
definitions.example: Mary sighed, knowing her ideals were far too romantic to work in reality.

definitions.definition: Having the qualities of romance (in the sense of something appealing deeply to the imagination); invoking on a powerfully sentimental idea of life; evocative, atmospheric.
Pertaining to an idealised form of love (originally, as might be felt by the heroes of a romance); conducive to romance; loving, affectionate.
definitions.example: Their kiss started casually, but it slowly turned romantic.

definitions.definition: Pliable material derived from the sap of the rubber tree; a hydrocarbon polymer of isoprene.
Synthetic materials with the same properties as natural rubber.
An eraser.
A condom.
Someone or something which rubs.
The cushion of an electric machine.
The rectangular pad on the pitcher's mound from which the pitcher must pitch.

The act of coming up.

A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.

The edible white flesh of this fruit.
The coconut palm.
A Hispanic or dark-skinned person who acts “white” (Caucasian), alluding to the fact that a coconut is brown on the outside and white on the inside.
A black person who thinks or acts "white" (European).
A Pacific islander.
A female breast.
The human head (often used in cricket broadcasts when a ball hits or nearly hits a batsman on the head).
An office holder invested with powers and authorities.
definitions.example: David Barnes was the official charged with the running of the sports club.

definitions.definition: A person responsible for applying the rules of a game or sport in a competition.
definitions.example: In most soccer games there are three officials: the referee and two linesmen.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to an office or public trust.

definitions.definition: Derived from the proper office or officer, or from the proper authority; made or communicated by virtue of authority

definitions.definition: Approved by authority; authorized.

definitions.definition: (pharmaceutical) Sanctioned by the pharmacopoeia; appointed to be used in medicine; officinal.
definitions.example: an official drug or preparation

definitions.definition: Discharging an office or function.
Relating to an office; especially, to a subordinate executive officer or attendant.
Relating to an ecclesiastical judge appointed by a bishop, chapter, archdeacon, etc., with charge of the spiritual jurisdiction.
True, real, beyond doubt.

definitions.definition: Listen in a national pharmacopeia.
One who studies astronomy, the stars or the physical universe; a scientist whose area of research is astronomy or astrophysics
In any way.
Anyway, anyhow, in any case.
A bound collection of maps often including tables, illustrations or other text.
A bound collection of tables, illustrations etc. on any given subject.

(especially of the human body) A detailed visual conspectus of something of great and multi-faceted complexity, with its elements splayed so as to be presented in as discrete a manner as possible whilst retaining a realistic view of the whole.

A collection of top-dimensional subspaces, called charts, each homeomorphic to Euclidean space, which comprise the entirety of a manifold, such that intersecting charts' respective homeomorphisms are compatible in a certain way.

A figure of a man used as a column; telamon.
A sheet of paper measuring 26 inches by 34 inches.
The deliberate giving of false or misleading testimony under oath.

definitions.definition: A foolish and embarrassing error, especially one made in public.
A single thickness of some material covering a surface.
definitions.example: After the first coat of paint dried, he applied another layer.

definitions.definition: A (usually) horizontal deposit; a stratum.
definitions.example: I find seven-layer cake a bit too rich.

definitions.definition: One of the items in a hierarchy.
definitions.example: mired in layers of deceit

definitions.definition: (by analogy to a stack of transparencies) one in a stack of (initially transparent) drawing surfaces that comprise an image; used to keep elements of an image separate so that they can be modified independently from one another.
To cut or divide (something) into layers
To arrange (something) in layers.
definitions.example: Layer the ribbons on top of one another to make an attractive pattern.

definitions.definition: Tending to excite wonder; surprising, extraordinary.
Surprisingly excellent; very good or admirable, extremely impressive.
definitions.example: They served a wonderful six-course meal.

definitions.definition: Exceedingly, to a great extent.
Like a dork.
A negative drug trip.
definitions.example: Normally those pills give me a boost, but last night they gave me a downer.

definitions.definition: A drug that has depressant qualities.
Something or someone disagreeable, dispiriting or depressing; a killjoy.
A livestock animal that has collapsed.
A form of industrial action in which workers down tools and refuse to work.
To accept as true, particularly without absolute certainty (i.e., as opposed to knowing)
definitions.example: I believe there are faeries.

definitions.definition: To accept that someone is telling the truth.
definitions.example: Why did I ever believe you?

definitions.definition: To have religious faith; to believe in a greater truth.
definitions.example: After that night in the church, I believed.

definitions.definition: To opine, think, reckon
A common gambling card game in casinos, where the object is to get as close to 21 without going over.
A hand in the game of blackjack consisting of a face card and an ace.
The flag (i.e., a jack) traditionally flown by pirate ships; popularly thought to be a white skull and crossed bones on a black field (the Jolly Roger).

A small, flat, blunt, usually leather-covered weapon loaded with heavy material such as lead or ball bearings, intended to inflict a blow to the head that renders the victim unconscious with diminished risk of lasting cranial trauma.
Any of several species of weed of genus Bidens, such as Bidens pilosa, in the family Compositae.
A blackjack oak.
To strike with a blackjack or similar weapon.
To make ill.
definitions.example: The infection will sicken him until amputation is needed.

definitions.definition: To become ill.
definitions.example: I will sicken if I don’t get some more exercise.

definitions.definition: To fill with disgust or abhorrence.
definitions.example: His arrogant behaviour sickens me.

definitions.definition: To lower the standing of.
To be filled with disgust or abhorrence.
To become disgusting or tedious.
To become weak; to decay; to languish.
A monocular optical instrument that magnifies distant objects, especially in astronomy.
Any instrument used in astronomy for observing distant objects (such as a radio telescope).
To extend or contract in the manner of a telescope.
To slide or pass one within another, after the manner of the sections of a small telescope or spyglass.
To come into collision, as railway cars, in such a manner that one runs into another.
A deal to swap goods or services.
A conservative; originally tied to Nicaraguan counter-revolutionaries.
An entry (or account) that cancels another entry (or account).
definitions.example: The sum for each contra account is shown in the last column.

definitions.definition: Any of the musical instruments in the contrabass range, e.g. contrabassoon, contrabass clarinet or, especially, double bass.
A contra dance.
A country dance.
One who counts
definitions.example: He's only 16 months, but is already a good counter – he can count to 100.

definitions.definition: A reckoner; someone who collects data by counting; an enumerator.
An object (now especially a small disc) used in counting or keeping count, or as a marker in games, etc.
definitions.example: He rolled a six on the dice, so moved his counter forward six spaces.

definitions.definition: A telltale; a contrivance attached to an engine, printing press, or other machine, for the purpose of counting the revolutions or the pulsations.
A variable, memory location, etc. whose contents are incremented to keep a count.
A hit counter.
A table or board on which money is counted and over which business is transacted
definitions.example: He put his money on the counter, and the shopkeeper put it in the till.

definitions.definition: A shop tabletop on which goods are examined, weighed or measured.
In a kitchen, a surface, often built into the wall and above a cabinet, designed to be used for food preparation.
In a bathroom, a surface, often built into the wall and above a cabinet, which holds the washbasin.
Any stone lying closer to the center than any of the opponent's stones.
The prison attached to a city court; a compter.

(grammar) A class of word used along with numbers to count objects and events, typically mass nouns. Although rare and optional in English (e.g. "20 head of cattle"), they are numerous and required in Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
Something opposite or contrary to something else.
A proactive defensive hold or move in reaction to a hold or move by one's opponent.
definitions.example: Always know a counter to any hold you try against your opponent.

definitions.definition: The overhanging stern of a vessel above the waterline, below and somewhat forward of the stern proper.
The piece of a shoe or a boot around the heel of the foot (above the heel of the shoe/boot).
The breast of a horse; that part of a horse between the shoulders and under the neck.
The enclosed or partly closed negative space of a glyph.
An encounter.
To contradict, oppose.
To return a blow while receiving one, as in boxing.
To take action in response to; to respond.
To encounter.
To steal an automobile forcibly from (someone).
definitions.example: Someone should carjack that pompous jerk and teach him a lesson!

definitions.definition: To forcibly steal (a vehicle).
definitions.example: These twerps have carjacked their last Mercedes.

definitions.definition: To combine with oxygen or otherwise make an oxide.
To increase the valence (or the positive charge) of an element by removing electrons.
To coat something with an oxide.
To become oxidized.
To make something fluffy.
definitions.example: The cat fluffed its tail.

definitions.definition: To become fluffy, puff up.
To move lightly like fluff.
(of an actor or announcer) To make a mistake in one's lines.
To do incorrectly, for example mishit, miskick, miscue etc.
To fart.
To arouse (a male pornographic actor) before filming.
The visible part of fire; a stream of burning vapour or gas, emitting light and heat.
A romantic partner or lover in a usually short-lived but passionate affair.
Intentionally insulting criticism or remark meant to incite anger.
A brilliant reddish orange-gold fiery colour.
The contrasting light and dark figure seen in wood used for stringed instrument making; the curl.
definitions.example: The cello has a two-piece back with a beautiful narrow flame.

definitions.definition: Burning zeal, passion, imagination, excitement, or anger.
To produce flames; to burn with a flame or blaze.
To burst forth like flame; to break out in violence of passion; to be kindled with zeal or ardour.
To post a destructively critical or abusive message (to somebody).
definitions.example: I flamed him for spamming in my favourite newsgroup.

definitions.definition: Of a brilliant reddish orange-gold colour, like that of a flame.
An SI unit of energy equal to 103 joules. Symbol: kJ
A small board with keys primarily used for tactile input into a machine.
definitions.example: Many laptop computers have special function keys which turn part of the alphabetical keyboard into a numeric keypad.

definitions.definition: A covered vehicle used for carrying goods or people, usually roughly cuboid in shape, longer and higher than a car but smaller than a truck/lorry.
definitions.example: The van sped down the road.

definitions.definition: An enclosed railway vehicle for transport of goods.
A light wagon, either covered or open, used by tradesmen and others for the transportation of goods.
A large towable vehicle equipped for the repair of structures that cannot easily be moved.
To transport in a van or similar vehicle (especially of horses).
(used in passive voice) Of law enforcement: to arrest (not necessarily in a van; derived from party van).
A brief, unspecified amount of time.
definitions.example: Wait a moment, while I lock the front door.

definitions.definition: The smallest portion of time; an instant.
Weight or importance.
The turning effect of a force applied to a rotational system at a distance from the axis of rotation.
(unit) A definite period of time, specifically one-tenth of a point, or one-fortieth or one-fiftieth of an hour.
A petit mal episode; such a spell.
A fit; a brief tantrum.
An infinitesimal change in a varying quantity; an increment or decrement.
A quantitative measure of the shape of a set of points.
definitions.example: If the points represent mass, then the zeroth moment is the total mass, the first moment divided by the total mass is the center of mass, and the second moment is the rotational inertia.

definitions.definition: To agree; to be equal; to correspond.
definitions.example: Their interests didn't match, so it took a long time to agree what to do together.

definitions.definition: To agree with; to be equal to; to correspond to.
definitions.example: His interests didn't match her interests.

definitions.definition: To make a successful match or pairing.
definitions.example: They found out about his color-blindness when he couldn't match socks properly.

definitions.definition: To equal or exceed in achievement.
definitions.example: She matched him at every turn: anything he could do, she could do as well or better.

definitions.definition: To unite in marriage, to mate.
To fit together, or make suitable for fitting together; specifically, to furnish with a tongue and groove at the edges.
definitions.example: to match boards

definitions.definition: Short for matched grip.
Black bile, formerly thought to be one of the four "cardinal humours" of animal bodies.
Great sadness or depression, especially of a thoughtful or introspective nature.
Affected with great sadness or depression.
definitions.example: Melancholy people don't talk much.

definitions.definition: To mimic, to simulate.
To create an artistic representation of.
To make fun of by mimicking, to taunt.
To tantalise, and disappoint the hopes of.
To create a mockup or prototype of.
definitions.example: What's the best way to mock a database layer?

definitions.definition: Mockery
Derisive or contemptuous
Teasing or taunting
(of a sound) Of great intensity.
definitions.example: Turn that music down; it's too loud.

definitions.definition: (of a person, thing, event, etc.) Noisy.
definitions.example: a loud party that went on all night

definitions.definition: (of a person, event, etc.) Not subtle or reserved, brash.
(of clothing, decorations, etc.) Having unpleasantly and tastelessly contrasting colours or patterns; gaudy.
definitions.example: a loud style of dress;  loud colors

definitions.definition: (of marijuana) High-quality; premium; (by extension) having a strong or pungent odour indicating good quality
The act of speaking in a quiet voice, especially, without vibration of the vocal cords.
(usually in the plural) A rumor.
definitions.example: There are whispers of rebellion all around.

definitions.definition: A faint trace or hint (of something).
definitions.example: The soup had just a whisper of basil.

definitions.definition: A low rustling sound, like that of the wind in leaves.
A private message to an individual in a chat room.

To speak softly, or under the breath, so as to be heard only by one near at hand; to utter words without sonant breath; to talk without that vibration in the larynx which gives sonorous, or vocal, sound.
To mention privately and confidentially, or in a whisper.
To make a low, sibilant sound.
To speak with suspicion or timorous caution; to converse in whispers, as in secret plotting.
To address in a whisper, or low voice.
To prompt secretly or cautiously; to inform privately.
A high military officer in a warlike nation.
A local ruler or bandit leader usually where the government is weak.
To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.
definitions.example: The rider jumped the horse over the fence.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.
To increase the height of a tower crane by inserting a section at the base of the tower and jacking up everything above it.
To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.
(smithwork) To join by a buttweld.
To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.
To start executing code from a different location, rather than following the program counter.
To flee; to make one's escape.
A basket.
A type of cream cheese, originally made in a rush basket; later, a food made of sweetened curds or rennet.
A delicacy.
A feast or banquet.
A pleasure-trip; a journey made for feasting or enjoyment, now especially a trip made ostensibly for business but which entails merrymaking or entertainment.
A press junket.
A gaming room for which the capacity and limits change daily, often rented out to private vendors who run tour groups through them and give a portion of the proceeds to the main casino.
To attend a junket; to feast.
To go on a junket; to travel.
To regale or entertain with a feast.
A money prize pool which accumulates until the conditions are met for it to be won.
A large cash prize or money.
An unexpected windfall or reward.
A concentration of surface wood or fuel.
definitions.example: a juniper jackpot

definitions.definition: A large accumulated point bonus, originally awarded after a long set of actions but now often easily available in multiball modes.
(of a slot machine) To issue a jackpot.
Any of several white, often dark backed birds of the family Laridae having long pointed wings and short legs.
The symbol ̼ , which combines under a letter as a sort of accent.
A fan or member of Brighton and Hove Albion Football Club.
(of a forward) To run in the back line rather than concentrate on primary positional duties in open play.
To use a British Seagull outboard.
To work as a non-union casual stevedore.
To cover the roof with straw, reed, leaves, etc.
Something that is rejected.
An unpopular person.
A rejected defective product in a production line
To refuse to accept.
definitions.example: She even rejected my improved offer.

definitions.definition: To block a shot, especially if it sends the ball off the court.
To refuse a romantic advance.
definitions.example: I've been rejected three times this week.

definitions.definition: Not dependable.
The process of flowing out
To flow outward.
(of time) To take or use.
To take or use space.
To have sexual intercourse with.
To do business in; to busy oneself with.
To use; to expend; to make use of.
To persist steadfastly in pursuit of an undertaking, task, journey, or goal, even if hindered by distraction, difficulty, obstacles, or discouragement.
To stay constant; to continue in a certain state; to remain.
Protective armor for the hands, formerly thrown down as a challenge to combat.
A long glove covering the wrist.
A rope on which hammocks or clothes are hung for drying.
Two parallel rows of attackers who strike at a criminal as punishment
Simultaneous attack from two or more sides
Any challenging, difficult, or painful ordeal, often one performed for atonement or punishment
A temporary convergence of two parallel railroad tracks allowing passage through a narrow opening in each direction without switching.
A beginner; a person who is new to a subject, skill, or belief.
A novice (recent convert); a new convert or proselyte; a new monk.

A name given by the early Christians, and still given by the Roman Catholics, to those who have recently embraced the Christian faith, and been admitted to baptism, especially those converts from heathenism or Judaism.
A plant species recently introduced to an area (in contrast to archaeophyte, a long-established introduced species).
A demon or monster, thought to plague people while they slept and cause a feeling of suffocation and terror during sleep.
Sleep paralysis.
A very bad or frightening dream.
definitions.example: I had a nightmare that I tried to run but could neither move nor breathe.

definitions.definition: Any bad, miserable, difficult or terrifying situation or experience that arouses anxiety, terror, agony or great displeasure.
definitions.example: Cleaning up after identity theft can be a nightmare of phone calls and letters.

definitions.definition: The ability of the brain to record information or impressions with the facility of recalling them later at will.
definitions.example: Memory is a facility common to all animals.

definitions.definition: A record of a thing or an event stored and available for later use by the organism.
definitions.example: I have no memory of that event.

definitions.definition: The part of a computer that stores variable executable code or data (RAM) or unalterable executable code or default data (ROM).
definitions.example: This data passes from the CPU to the memory.

definitions.definition: The time within which past events can be or are remembered.
definitions.example: in recent memory; in living memory

definitions.definition: (of a material) which returns to its original shape when heated
definitions.example: Memory metal; memory plastic.

definitions.definition: A memorial.
(collective) A term of venery for a social group of elephants, normally called a herd.
A spasmodic, nervous system disease brought on by the tetanus bacteria. It causes muscles to seize up and may cause death by suffocation.
A boat especially designed for saving the lives of shipwrecked people or people in distress at sea (either launched from the shore with a crew, or else carried on board a larger ship)
An emergency vehicle carried aboard a spaceship
To rescue; to carry to safety
Riches; valuable material possessions.
A great amount; an abundance or plenty.
definitions.example: She brings a wealth of knowledge to the project.

definitions.definition: Prosperity; well-being; happiness.
A large, skin-covered drum played with the hands, used in West African and some fusion music.
Fluently persuasive and articulate
Effective in expressing meaning by speech
